text.title: Churn Measurement, Health Scoring, and Antipatterns
text.summary: Canonical churn metric definitions, benchmarks, health score model, and common mistakes that invalidate churn analysis.

rule.statement: Define churn once in writing with cancel-date semantics and grace-period rules; pin that definition in every dashboard description.
rule.rationale: Ambiguity in "churn date" (cancel event vs end-of-paid-period vs failed-payment threshold) produces different numbers in different tools and makes cross-team reporting meaningless.

rule.statement: Always report voluntary and involuntary churn separately.
rule.rationale: Involuntary churn (failed payments) is recoverable with dunning and smart retries — it has different root causes and different fixes than voluntary cancellation. Lumping them hides a fixable revenue leak.

rule.statement: Report customer churn alongside NRR; never report NRR alone in an expansion-led business.
rule.rationale: NRR above 100% can mask high logo churn when expansion MRR from surviving accounts exceeds lost MRR from churned accounts. SaaS investors look at NRR >= 110% (good) and >= 120% (great) but always alongside logo churn.

list.item: Customer churn rate = customers lost this month / customers at start of month
MRR churn rate = lost MRR this month / MRR at start of month
Net Revenue Retention = (start MRR + expansion MRR - churned MRR) / start MRR
Annual churn from monthly: 1 - (1 - monthly_rate)^12 (non-linear — do not linearly extrapolate)

rule.statement: Backtest health-score thresholds against last 90 days of actual churn before trusting the alerts.
rule.rationale: Health scores derived from short windows drift fast after product changes. An unvalidated score will fire false alerts and train teams to ignore it. Retro-fit threshold values against known churners before deploying to CS teams.

example.note: Composite health score combining four signals, each 0-25 points:
example.code:
Health Score = ( login_frequency_score (0-25) + feature_usage_score (0-25) + support_sentiment (0-25) + account_age_score (0-25) ) / 100
Thresholds (validate against actual churn): < 40 = At-risk → CS outreach 40-70 = Needs attention → automated nudge > 70 = Healthy

list.item: Not using product: 25-30% of churn, preventable with activation fix
Not seeing value: 20-25%, preventable with onboarding or success team
Budget/price: 15-20%, partially preventable with pricing tiers

antipattern.description: Only measuring churn rate without leading indicators.
antipattern.reason: Churn rate is a lagging metric — the customer has already left. Leading indicators (login frequency drop, feature usage reduction, support frustration) provide a 2-4 week window for intervention.

antipattern.description: Annualizing monthly churn by multiplying by 12.
antipattern.reason: Monthly churn compounds non-linearly. At 5% monthly, annual churn is 46%, not 60%. Linear extrapolation overstates the problem and distorts forecasts.

antipattern.description: Running health-score alerts without backtesting thresholds.
antipattern.reason: Unvalidated thresholds produce high false-positive rates. CS teams start ignoring alerts within weeks, making the entire system useless.

text.reference: Voluntary vs involuntary customer + MRR churn SQL
